summary: File-backed session storage implementing gotd's session.Storage interface, persisting session bytes as JSON-encoded data with 0600 permissions

requires: Parent directory of Path must exist before StoreSession is called (created by client.go:Run)
guarantees: Returns session.ErrNotFound (gotd sentinel) when file is absent — not a generic os error

effects: Reads from and writes to filesystem at configured path

observations.text: Session bytes are JSON-marshaled on store and unmarshaled on load — the byte slice becomes a base64 JSON string on disk. This is a deliberate single-level encoding, not a double-encode bug.
Returns session.ErrNotFound specifically (not io.EOF or os.ErrNotExist) — gotd's client.Run() relies on this sentinel to distinguish 'no session yet' from a real I/O error
No file locking; concurrent processes sharing the same session path can produce a torn write or read a partially written session
